purpose: define the bounded stitching policy that connects continuation chains on top of the existing bounded slice planner

problem_statement: multi-input continuation is still too single-input biased at execution time
alias-result stitching is not yet a first-class execution family
planner bridge families and propagation/witness execution families are still partly misaligned
edge provenance alone is too weak to explain which continuation chain actually won

goal: define bounded continuation stitching on top of the selected bounded slice
treat multi-input continuation as an input-binding problem rather than only a summary-input-count problem
treat alias-result stitching as an independent bridge family
define the minimal bridge-execution provenance needed for later G12 tasks
non_goal: full parity across all languages in this task alone
replacing the existing bounded slice planner

scope_planner_responsibility: stop expansion at the selected bounded slice
stitching_policy_responsibility: connect selected files and symbols into bounded continuation chains
choose input bindings, summary bridges, nested bridges, and alias-result stitches
report the winning continuation chain compactly

terms.stitch_ready_anchor: a selected direct-boundary, bridge-completion, or bridge-continuation symbol that can start a bounded continuation chain
terms.bounded_stitching_chain: a bounded ordered list of continuation execution steps built strictly within the selected bounded slice
terms.binding_map: a mapping from caller-side input nodes to callee summary input nodes, with explicit binding kinds
terms.coarse_chain_family: a compact family label used for representative selection, budgeting, and reporting

step_families.role: bind caller-side uses to callee summary inputs before continuation proceeds
carry selected summary output/impact back toward the caller result
continue the chain through a nested callee without collapsing back to a single-input fallback
carry an imported or nested result through temp/alias/result zones across wrapper and caller scopes
record Ruby require_relative continuity as part of a mixed continuation chain

rules: single-input cases still use the same binding-map contract
multi-input continuation must not treat zip-by-order as the canonical semantic contract
later bridge steps consume only the relevant subset selected by the binding map
unknown or literal companion slots are tracked explicitly instead of being silently ignored

chain_construction.name: extract_stitch_ready_anchor
rules: prefer selected bridge-continuation symbols first, then selected bridge-completion symbols, then direct-boundary symbols
same-seed same-family duplicates collapse to the representative that already won selected-vs-pruned reasoning

chain_construction.name: build_binding_map
rules: construct a binding map before summary or nested bridging begins
reordered, repeated, and partial bindings remain explicit rather than being normalized away
single-input fallback does not erase the binding-map abstraction

chain_construction.name: apply_summary_and_nested_bridges
rules: summary bridges consume only the binding-map subset that is actually selected
nested summaries may remain multi-input and must not immediately fall back to a single-input-only contract
caller-result bridges should be attached only for the relevant bound inputs

chain_construction.name: apply_alias_result_stitch
rules: alias-result stitching is a distinct step family, not just an accidental side-effect of summary return bridging
wrapper-local and caller-local alias zones may both participate in the same bounded chain
return-family and alias-result-family steps may coexist in one chain when needed

chain_construction.name: record_require_relative_load_when_needed
rules: Ruby mixed cases may include require_relative_load in the same chain as alias_result_stitch
require_relative provenance participates in chain reporting even when it is not itself a data-flow step

hard_rules: stitching never reopens files or symbols outside the selected bounded slice
pruned candidates are not revived by stitching execution
scope widening remains a planner responsibility, not a stitching responsibility
depth_policy: one alias-result stitching segment per locality
at most one coarse family transition inside the representative chain

directly_targets_cases.policy_value: binding-map-first continuation instead of single-input fallback
distinct alias-result stitch steps across wrapper and caller alias zones
mixed require_relative and alias-result chain reporting

needs_planner_followup.reason: the stitching policy can explain how to chain it once selected, but planner-side family-aware continuation still has to retain the alias-family representative in scope

fixed_decisions: scope and stitching stay separate responsibilities
multi-input continuation is handled as a binding-map problem
alias-result stitching is a first-class execution family
continuation chains are represented as bounded step-family sequences
stitching stays strictly inside the selected bounded slice
representative selection uses family-aware budgets rather than only path-count budgets
bridge execution provenance must include compact chain-level reporting in addition to raw edge provenance
